overview: The TTX Joiner step re-joins multiple TTX documents that were previously split (by the TTX Splitter Step) back into a single document. It takes raw documents as input and sends raw documents as output. The step outputs the resulting file in the same directory as the input documents, sorting input files internally to ensure proper part ordering before concatenation.

suffix.description: A marker text to add at the end of the output filename. For example, if set to '_CONCAT', an input file 'index.html_part001.ttx' would produce 'index.html_CONCAT.ttx'. If a file with the same name already exists in the output directory, it will be overridden without warning.
notes: If a file with the same name already exists in the output directory, it will be overridden without warning.

limitations: This step is triggered at the END_BATCH event.
Input files with no part marker, or invalid part marker will be ignored (with a warning).
The received event is unaltered — the step only outputs the resulting joined file to the same directory as the input documents.
processingNotes: The input file list is sorted internally before processing so all parts of each original document are listed in the proper order.
The step outputs the resulting file in the same directory as the input documents.
Input files with no part marker or invalid part marker will be ignored with a warning.

examples.title: Joining split TTX files with a suffix
examples.description: Demonstrates how multiple split TTX part files are sorted and joined back into their original documents with a custom suffix appended to the filename.
examples.output:
Files are first sorted internally:
  C:\Project\FolderABC\index.html_part001.ttx
  C:\Project\FolderABC\index.html_part002.ttx
  C:\Project\FolderABC\someFile.html_part001.ttx
  C:\Project\FolderABC\someFile.html_part002.ttx

Output files:
  C:\Project\FolderABC\index.html_CONCAT.ttx
  C:\Project\FolderABC\someFile.html_CONCAT.ttx
